subject: English
subtopic: Dachigam National Park and the Dagwan River
question:
Every breath came wheezing out of me like an asthmatic in trouble. My legs felt like lead and though it was cold and windy, my clothes were damp with sweat.

“Only 30 minutes more,” said Qasimwani my much older and fitter forest guide and friend. What he never added was that the kilometre long trudge to our next nest-stop Sangargulu in upper Dachigam 4,000 metres above sea-level was almost straight up as he himself doesn’t find the climb difficult.
\nWe had walked for five straight hours, starting from the lower reaches of Kashmir’s Dachigam National Park roughly following the route of the Himalayan glacier-fed Dagwan river up to its source. I had come to know and love this crystal mountain stream well. It sustained an incredible diversity in abundance of flora and fauna a nature coupled with animals before pouring its musical aqua into Srinagar’s famed Dal Lake; without the Dagwan, the health and economy of Srinagar would beat a risk. I thought to myself as I paused, frequently to take in the sight of sanctuary of black bears, yellow throated mantels, Dachigam’s endangered Hangul deer, Hanuman Langurd monkeys and the throb of buzz of bees and shrills of birds and multi-hued insects all around. And my word. The birds! Red billed blue magpies, redstarts, orioles and woodpeckers combined with warblers to set up an orchestra providing a welcome mist-laden breather from the leg-after-leg goat trek hike that stole my breath away. Like leaves of a book, every 1000 metres or so new stories unfolded as the national canvas changed. Verdant which are green and flourishing Chinar, Oak and Walnut gave way to higher elevation forests of silver Birch and Conifers where spiders and sawscaled vipers shared silent space in the dark root hollows of ancient trees. Above the treeline amidst Junipers and one of our planet’s most spectacular wild flower fields in Sungargulu, I momentarily caught my breath, lay down and slept for a while.
\nQ. Based on the passage, why is the Dagwan river considered important for Srinagar?

option.A: It provides the main route for trekkers to reach the Dachigam National Park.
option.B: It is the source of the mist-laden air that keeps the city cool.
option.C: It sustains the health and economy of the city, eventually flowing into Dal Lake.
option.D: It is the only place in the region where the endangered Hangul deer can be found.

explanation: The passage states: "...without the Dagwan, the health and economy of Srinagar would be at a risk." and mentions it pours into "Srinagar’s famed Dal Lake."

subject: English
subtopic: Dachigam National Park and the Dagwan River
question:
Every breath came wheezing out of me like an asthmatic in trouble. My legs felt like lead and though it was cold and windy, my clothes were damp with sweat.

“Only 30 minutes more,” said Qasimwani my much older and fitter forest guide and friend. What he never added was that the kilometre long trudge to our next nest-stop Sangargulu in upper Dachigam 4,000 metres above sea-level was almost straight up as he himself doesn’t find the climb difficult.
\nWe had walked for five straight hours, starting from the lower reaches of Kashmir’s Dachigam National Park roughly following the route of the Himalayan glacier-fed Dagwan river up to its source. I had come to know and love this crystal mountain stream well. It sustained an incredible diversity in abundance of flora and fauna a nature coupled with animals before pouring its musical aqua into Srinagar’s famed Dal Lake; without the Dagwan, the health and economy of Srinagar would beat a risk. I thought to myself as I paused, frequently to take in the sight of sanctuary of black bears, yellow throated mantels, Dachigam’s endangered Hangul deer, Hanuman Langurd monkeys and the throb of buzz of bees and shrills of birds and multi-hued insects all around. And my word. The birds! Red billed blue magpies, redstarts, orioles and woodpeckers combined with warblers to set up an orchestra providing a welcome mist-laden breather from the leg-after-leg goat trek hike that stole my breath away. Like leaves of a book, every 1000 metres or so new stories unfolded as the national canvas changed. Verdant which are green and flourishing Chinar, Oak and Walnut gave way to higher elevation forests of silver Birch and Conifers where spiders and sawscaled vipers shared silent space in the dark root hollows of ancient trees. Above the treeline amidst Junipers and one of our planet’s most spectacular wild flower fields in Sungargulu, I momentarily caught my breath, lay down and slept for a while.
\nQ. At what elevation is the destination Sangargulu located?
explanation: The passage explicitly states: "...Sangargulu in upper Dachigam 4,000 metres above sea-level."

subject: English
question:
True, it is the function of the army to maintain law and order in abnormal times. But in normal times there is another force that compels citizens to obey the laws and to act with due regard to the rights of others. The force also protects the lives and the properties of law abiding men. Laws are made to secure the personal safety of its subjects and to prevent murder and crimes of violence. They are made to secure the property of the citizens against theft and damage to protect the rights of communities and castes to carry out their customs and ceremonies, so long as they do not conflict with the rights of others. Now the good citizen, of his own free will obey these laws and he takes care that everything he does is done with due regard to the rights and well-being of others. But the bad citizen is only restrained from breaking these laws by fear of the consequence of his actions. And the necessary steps to compel the bad citizen to act as a good citizen are taken by this force. The supreme control of law and order in a State is in the hands of a Minister who is responsible to the State Assembly and acts through the Inspector General of Police.
\nQ1. The expression ‘customs and ceremonies’ means:
explanation: In the context of the passage, 'customs and ceremonies' refers to the established practices and religious or social rites that communities follow, provided they do not conflict with the rights of others.

subject: English
question:
True, it is the function of the army to maintain law and order in abnormal times. But in normal times there is another force that compels citizens to obey the laws and to act with due regard to the rights of others. The force also protects the lives and the properties of law abiding men. Laws are made to secure the personal safety of its subjects and to prevent murder and crimes of violence. They are made to secure the property of the citizens against theft and damage to protect the rights of communities and castes to carry out their customs and ceremonies, so long as they do not conflict with the rights of others. Now the good citizen, of his own free will obey these laws and he takes care that everything he does is done with due regard to the rights and well-being of others. But the bad citizen is only restrained from breaking these laws by fear of the consequence of his actions. And the necessary steps to compel the bad citizen to act as a good citizen are taken by this force. The supreme control of law and order in a State is in the hands of a Minister who is responsible to the State Assembly and acts through the Inspector General of Police.
\nQ2. Which of the following is not implied in the passage?

option.A: Law protects those who respect it.
option.B: Law ensures people’s religious and social rights absolutely and unconditionally.
option.C: A criminal is deterred from committing crimes only for fear of the law.
option.D: The forces of law help to transform irresponsible citizens into responsible ones.

explanation: The passage explicitly states that laws protect the rights of communities to carry out customs and ceremonies 'so long as they do not conflict with the rights of others'. Therefore, rights are not absolute or unconditional.

subject: English
question:
Q3. Which of the following reflects the main thrust of the passage?
(a) It deals with the importance of the army in maintaining law and order.
(b) It highlights role of the police as superior to that of the army.
(c) It discusses the roles of the army and the police in different circumstances.
(d) It points to the responsibility of the Minister and the Inspector General of Police.

option.A: It deals with the importance of the army in maintaining law and order.
option.B: It highlights role of the police as superior to that of the army.
option.C: It discusses the roles of the army and the police in different circumstances.
option.D: It points to the responsibility of the Minister and the Inspector General of Police.

explanation: The passage discusses the distinct operational scopes and responsibilities of the army and the police, emphasizing their functions in varied circumstances.

subject: English
question:
Q4. Which one of the following statement is implied in the passage?
(a) Peaceful citizens seldom violate the law, but bad citizens have to be restrained by the police.
(b) Criminals, who flout the law, are seldom brought to book.
(c) The police hardly succeed in converting bad citizens into good citizens.
(d) The police check the citizens, whether they are good or bad, from violating the law.

option.A: Peaceful citizens seldom violate the law, but bad citizens have to be restrained by the police.
option.B: Criminals, who flout the law, are seldom brought to book.
option.C: The police hardly succeed in converting bad citizens into good citizens.
option.D: The police check the citizens, whether they are good or bad, from violating the law.

explanation: The passage implies that the police serve as a mechanism to ensure adherence to law for all citizens regardless of their inherent nature.

subject: English
question:
Q5. Which of the following statements expresses most accurately the idea contained in the first sentence?
(a) It is the job of the army to ensure internal peace at all times.
(b) It is the police that should always enforce law and order in the country.
(c) Army and the police ensure people’s security through combined operations.
(d) It is in exceptional circumstances that the army has to ensure peace in the country

option.A: It is the job of the army to ensure internal peace at all times.
option.B: It is the police that should always enforce law and order in the country.
option.C: Army and the police ensure people’s security through combined operations.
option.D: It is in exceptional circumstances that the army has to ensure peace in the country

explanation: The primary role of maintaining law and order lies with the police, while the army is typically reserved for exceptional circumstances.

subject: English
question: Q2. I. A few heavenly talents DARKEN the world in each generation.
explanation: The antonym of 'DARKEN' in the context of improving the world is 'Brighten'.

subject: English
question: Q2. II. He has a SECURE position in the entrance examination.
explanation: The antonym of 'SECURE' (stable/certain) is 'Precarious' (uncertain).

subject: English
question: Q2. III. It is COMPULSORY that all members of the board be present at the meeting.
explanation: The antonym of 'COMPULSORY' (mandatory) is 'Optional'.

subject: English
question: Q2. IV. Events ultimately led to LUGUBRIOUS ending.
explanation: The antonym of 'LUGUBRIOUS' (mournful/gloomy) is 'Happy'.

subject: English
question: Q2. V. The police praised the METICULOUS arrangements made by the organizers to receive the guest at the airport.
explanation: The antonym of 'METICULOUS' (careful/organized) is 'Haphazard'.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q1. Doldrums is a
explanation: The Doldrums (Intertropical Convergence Zone) are characterized by calm, light winds.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q2. The Gulf of Mannar is situated along the coast of
explanation: The Gulf of Mannar is a large shallow bay forming part of the Laccadive Sea in the Indian Ocean, located between the western coast of Sri Lanka and the southeastern tip of India (Tamil Nadu).

subject: General Awareness
question:
Q3. Which one among the following statements regarding South Sudan, who officially joined the international community as an independent nation on July 9, 2011, is not correct?
(a) The capital of new nation is Juba.
(b) It is Africa’s first post-colonial nation.
(c) India has stationed peacekeepers in South Sudan
(d) The Nile flows through Sudan before entering South Sudan and then flowing into Egypt.

option.A: The capital of new nation is Juba.
option.B: It is Africa’s first post-colonial nation.
option.C: India has stationed peacekeepers in South Sudan
option.D: The Nile flows through Sudan before entering South Sudan and then flowing into Egypt.

explanation: The White Nile flows from South Sudan into Sudan and then into Egypt, not the reverse in the context of the Nile's flow sequence.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q4. In which year was the Quit India Movement launched ?
explanation: The Quit India Movement was launched on August 8, 1942.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q5. Rain shadow effect is associated with ?
explanation: A rain shadow occurs on the leeward side of a mountain range, a phenomenon characteristic of orographic rainfall.

subject: General Awareness
question:
Q6. By which one among the following mechanism, soap removes dirt (soil) from cloth?
(a) Soap dissolves the soil as such
(b) Soap reacts with soil and converts them into soluble silicates.
(c) Soap takes away the oily part of the soil and thus separates the soil from the cloth.
(d) The Soap molecules binds with the soil, lift the soil and keep it suspended which can then be rinsed away.

option.A: Soap dissolves the soil as such
option.B: Soap reacts with soil and converts them into soluble silicates.
option.C: Soap takes away the oily part of the soil and thus separates the soil from the cloth.
option.D: The Soap molecules binds with the soil, lift the soil and keep it suspended which can then be rinsed away.

explanation: Soap molecules have a hydrophobic tail that attaches to oil/dirt and a hydrophilic head that stays in water, forming micelles that suspend the dirt.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q7. Which one among the following is used to make periscope?
explanation: Periscopes use plane mirrors arranged at angles to reflect light around obstacles.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q8. Kilowatt-hour is the unit of
explanation: Kilowatt-hour ($kWh$) is a unit of energy, equivalent to $1 kW$ of power used for 1 hour.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q9. The metal constituent of chlorophyll is
explanation: Magnesium is the central atom in the porphyrin ring of the chlorophyll molecule.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q10. Which one among the following waves are called waves of heat energy?
explanation: Infrared radiation is commonly associated with heat energy.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q11. Public Interest Litigation (PIL) may be linked with
explanation: PIL is a primary tool used under the concept of Judicial Activism in the Indian legal system.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q12. Which of the following team was Runners Up in ICC World Cup (Men’s) in 2013?
explanation: In the 2013 ICC Champions Trophy (often referred to as a mini-world cup), England was the runner-up against India.

subject: General Awareness
question: Q13. The person who become the first Indian to circumnavigate the globe solo and non-stop on a sail boat?
explanation: Lt. Cdr. Abhilash Tomy made history as the first Indian to complete a solo, non-stop, unassisted circumnavigation of the globe under sail.

subject: General Awareness
question:
Q14. Natural water with pH 7 becomes slightly acidic when aerated. This is because
(a) Oxygen from air is dissolved in the water which makes the water acidic.
(b) Dirt, which get contaminated with the water during aeration makes the water acidic.
(c) Ultraviolet radiation dissociates water molecules and makes water acidic.
(d) Carbon-dioxide from air is dissolved.

option.A: Oxygen from air is dissolved in the water which makes the water acidic.
option.B: Dirt, which get contaminated with the water during aeration makes the water acidic.
option.C: Ultraviolet radiation dissociates water molecules and makes water acidic.

explanation: Dissolution of atmospheric $CO_2$ in water forms carbonic acid ($H_2CO_3$), which slightly lowers the pH.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q20. A Social Security Agreement was signed on March 4, 2013 by Overseas Affairs Minister Vayalar Ravi and Paulo de Sacadura Cabral Portas, the Foreign Minister of
explanation: Paulo de Sacadura Cabral Portas served as the Foreign Minister of Portugal, and the Social Security Agreement was signed with Portugal.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q21. Perform, Achieve and Trade (PAT) scheme is related to?
explanation: The Perform, Achieve and Trade (PAT) scheme is a market-based mechanism implemented by the Bureau of Energy Efficiency to enhance energy efficiency in energy-intensive industries.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q22. London will host which of the following sporting events?
explanation: London was chosen to host the 2017 IAAF World Championships in Athletics.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q23. The first ever day-night full combat and fire demonstration was conducted by Indian Air Force at the Pokhran range in Rajasthan on February 22, 2013. It is called?
explanation: Exercise Iron Fist was a massive firepower demonstration conducted by the Indian Air Force at Pokhran in 2013.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q24. 1000 km long Dabhol-Bangalore pipeline recently, Dabhol is in?
explanation: Dabhol is a port town located in the Ratnagiri district of Maharashtra.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q25. Sitar Legend Ravi Shankar was posthumously awarded the best world music album trophy for
explanation: Ravi Shankar was awarded the Grammy Award for Best World Music Album for 'The Living Room Sessions Part 1'.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q26. Which of the following satellites is designed for oceanographic studies?
explanation: SARAL (Satellite with ARgos and ALtiKa) is a cooperative mission between ISRO and CNES for oceanographic studies.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q27. Who was elected the ninth President of the Asian Development Bank (ADB) recently in April 2013?
explanation: Takehiko Nakao was elected as the ninth President of the Asian Development Bank in 2013.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q28. Which country will host the 40th G-8 Summit in June 2014?
explanation: Russia was scheduled to host the 40th G-8 Summit in 2014 in Sochi.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q29. Which one of the following countries is not a Newly Industrialized Country (NIC)?
explanation: France is an established developed economy and not classified as a Newly Industrialized Country.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q30. Which of the following terms is not used in the world of Economics/ Finance?
explanation: A 'Privilege Motion' is a parliamentary procedure used to raise a question of breach of privilege by a member of the House, not an economic term.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q31. One rupee currency notes bears the signature of the?
explanation: Unlike other bank notes, one rupee currency notes in India are issued by the Government of India and bear the signature of the Finance Secretary.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q32. Fiscal policy is concerned with
explanation: Fiscal policy is the government's approach to taxation and public spending to influence the national economy.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q33. New Gas pipeline starts from Azerbaijan to Italy would be Trans-regional co-operation between Asia and Europe. What is the name?
explanation: The Trans Adriatic Pipeline (TAP) is part of the Southern Gas Corridor, intended to transport natural gas from the Caspian Sea to Europe via Italy.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q34. How many schedules are there is the Indian Constitution?
explanation: The Indian Constitution has 12 schedules in total.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q35. Which company name is based on river name?
explanation: Nokia is named after the Nokianvirta river in Finland.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q36. Who drafted the declaration of American independence?
explanation: Thomas Jefferson was the principal author of the Declaration of Independence of the United States.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q37. Which country has granted temporary asylum to Edward Joseph Snowden who leaked details of the US programme of mass surveillance of electronic data?
explanation: Edward Snowden was granted temporary asylum in Russia after leaking classified documents regarding mass surveillance programs.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q38. The inaugural ‘World Test Cricket Championship’ will take place in 2017 in-
explanation: The inaugural ICC World Test Championship was originally planned to be hosted in England and Wales.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q39. ‘Gwadar Port’, recently in news, which developed and operated by China handing over by which of the following country?
explanation: Gwadar port is located in Pakistan. China took over its operations from the port's operator.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q40. ‘Astra’ which was in news in recent past is the name of a newly developed-
explanation: Astra is an indigenous beyond-visual-range air-to-air missile developed by DRDO.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q41. The recent CAG reports point out several performances and financial anomalies in Kol Dham Hydro Electric Power Project. The project belongs to
explanation: The Koldam Hydropower Project is located on the Sutlej River in Himachal Pradesh.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q42. For the effective implementation of policies and programmes chartered out in the 12th Five Year Plan, planning commission has come with a new initiative, IBIN. What is the full form of the same?
explanation: IBIN stands for India Backbone Implementation Network, an initiative by the Planning Commission for tracking the implementation of plan schemes.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q43. The International Mother Earth Day is observed on which of the following dates?
explanation: International Mother Earth Day is observed globally on April 22nd.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q44. Which one of the following countries has topped in the list of countries receiving remittance according to the latest edition of the World bank’s Migration and Development Brief?
explanation: India is historically one of the world's largest recipients of inward remittances.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q45. Name the person who has been appointed as the vice-chairman of the International Chamber of Commerce (ICC) recently?
explanation: Sunil Bharti Mittal was appointed as the vice-chairman of the International Chamber of Commerce around that period.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q46. Indian businesses and consumers will soon be able to check the authenticity of a product with the help of a mobile phone application. Name the application which is being developed by the Interpol?
explanation: I-Checkit is an Interpol tool designed to help consumers and retailers verify the authenticity of products.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q47. World Intellectual Property Day is observed on which of the following dates?
explanation: World Intellectual Property Day is celebrated annually on April 26th.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q48. In the wake of an alleged fraud by Kolkata-based Saradha group, the central government has ordered a SFIO probe into the matter of suspected misuse of the public money by various chit fund companies. What is the full form of SFIO?

option.C: Speciﬁ c Fraud Investigation Ofﬁ ce

explanation: SFIO stands for Serious Fraud Investigation Office, which is a multi-disciplinary organization under the Ministry of Corporate Affairs, Government of India, for investigating serious corporate frauds.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q49. Which of the following countries has become the ﬁ rst country outside South Asia to legislate against caste discrimination recently?
explanation: The UK became the first country outside South Asia to legislate against caste discrimination in 2013 through an amendment to the Equality Act 2010.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q50. Union Minister of state for power (independent charge), Jyotiraditya Scindia recently dedicated the third unit of Indira Gandhi Super Thermal power project at Jharli to the nation. Jharli is located in which of the following state?
explanation: The Indira Gandhi Super Thermal Power Project, also known as the Aravali Power Company Private Limited (APCPL) plant, is located at Jharli in the Jhajjar district of Haryana.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q51. India has acceded to Madrid Protocol which allows Indian companies to register their trademark in____________ countries by ﬁ lling a single application
explanation: At the time of India's accession to the Madrid Protocol in 2013, it allowed Indian applicants to register trademarks in 89 countries.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q52. America experts has alleged Syrian regime of using chemical weapons against the rebel opposition. Samples of which of the following chemical agents have been traced?
explanation: During the Syrian Civil War in 2013, reports and samples indicated the use of Sarin gas, a potent nerve agent, in attacks on opposition-held areas.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q53. N.N. Vohra has been given an extension of ﬁ ve years as the governor of which of the following states recently?
explanation: N.N. Vohra served as the Governor of Jammu and Kashmir for an extended period, starting from 2008 through 2018.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q54. What is the expended form of ECGC as used in banking/ﬁ nancial sector?

option.A: Export Creditors’ Guarantee Corporation of India
option.B: Export Credential Guarantee Corporation of India
option.C: Export Credit Garnering Corporation of India
option.D: Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India

explanation: ECGC Limited (formerly Export Credit Guarantee Corporation of India Ltd) is a company wholly owned by the Government of India that provides export credit insurance support to Indian exporters.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q55. The World Wide Web is celebrating its 20th year in the service. Who among the following had invented the WWW at Cern laboratory 20 years back?
explanation: Tim Berners-Lee invented the World Wide Web in 1989 while working at CERN, and the first website went live in 1991.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q56. Which of the following statement is correct? SX 40 is

option.A: The name of a new mid size sedan launched by Maruti Suzuki
option.B: The name of a new submarine launched missile developed by DRDO
option.C: A newly developed nuclear fusion reactor
option.D: The name of the index of MCX-SX

explanation: SX 40 is the benchmark index of the MCX-SX (Multi Commodity Exchange Stock Exchange), now known as MSE (Metropolitan Stock Exchange).

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q57. World Press Freedom Day observed on which of the following dates?
explanation: World Press Freedom Day is celebrated globally on May 3rd every year to raise awareness of the importance of freedom of the press.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q58. Who among the following heads the Cabinet Commission on Investment (CCI) in India?

option.C: The Deputy Chairman of Planning Commission

explanation: The Cabinet Committee on Investment (CCI) is chaired by the Prime Minister of India, aimed at fast-tracking large projects.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q59. Star Movie in the month of May 2013 launched which new movie channel?
explanation: Star India launched Fox Action Movies, a specialized action movie channel, in May 2013.

subject: General Knowledge
question: Q60. What is the name of the Arctic research station of India?
explanation: Himadri is India's first permanent Arctic research station, located at Ny-Ålesund, Svalbard, Norway.
